content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To     : ALL OPINION REQUEST MEETING                    Date  : August 31, 1976
         PARTICIPANTS
                                                               M-76-321

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ken Finney

Subject: Closing date for postelection campaign
         statements -- Section 84205


         At a previous opinion request meeting, we discussed whether
a committee should be permitted to file its postelection campaign
statement early when all contributions and expenditures with
respect to the election to which the statement will apply have
been made, but the committee will continue to receive and/or make
contributions and to make expenditures prior to the closing date
established by Section 84205 (albeit with respect to a different
election). The main problem created by permitting an early filing
is that the committee may close its books early but another com-
mittee to which it makes a contribution prior to the closing date
might keep its books open until the specified closing date. If
this occurs, the transaction would be reported on the recipient
committee's postelection statement but not on the donor commit-
tee's postelection statement. To the contrary, the donor commit-
tee would report the contribution on its first preelection state-
ment for the next election. In light of this problem, it was
determined that strict adherence to the closing dates specified
in the statute is desirable. Accordingly, at the present time,
the staff should inform committees that they may not close their
books prior to the specified closing date if they anticipate any
further contributions or expenditures during the postelection
period established by the statute.

         In the meantime, the staff will investigate the feasibility
of interpreting the last sentence of Section 84205 to read, in
effect: "if all liabilities of the filer have been paid and no
additional contributions or expenditures are anticipated with
respect to the election for which the campaign statement is
being filed." An interpretation of Section 84205 which reads
into the statute the underlined portion of the foregoing quote
would permit an early filing under the circumstances outlined
above.


KF:cjb

sections.question_synthetic: Whether political committees may file postelection campaign statements early when all contributions and expenditures for a specific election are complete, even if the committee will continue to receive or make contributions and expenditures for different elections prior to the statutory closing date.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC staff determined that strict adherence to statutory closing dates in Section 84205 is required, and committees may not close their books early if they anticipate further contributions or expenditures during the postelection period, even if those contributions/expenditures relate to a different election. The staff is investigating whether Section 84205 could be interpreted to permit early filing only when all liabilities are paid and no additional contributions or expenditures are anticipated with respect to that specific election.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an internal FPPC memorandum to opinion request meeting participants rather than a formal advice letter to an external requester. No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; content was synthesized from narrative discussion. Dated August 31, 1976 and addresses Section 84205 of California campaign finance law.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether political committees may file postelection campaign statements early when all contributions and expenditures for a specific election are complete, even if the committee will continue to receive or make contributions and expenditures for different elections prior to the statutory closing date.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC staff determined that strict adherence to statutory closing dates in Section 84205 is required, and committees may not close their books early if they anticipate further contributions or expenditures during the postelection period, even if those contributions/expenditures relate to a different election. The staff is investigating whether Section 84205 could be interpreted to permit early filing only when all liabilities are paid and no additional contributions or expenditures are anticipated with respect to that specific election.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : ALL OPINION REQUEST MEETING Date : August 31, 1976 PARTICIPANTS M-76-321 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Ken Finney Subject: Closing date for postelection campaign statements -- Section 84205 At a previous opinion request meeting, we discussed whether a committee should be permitted to file its postelection campaign statement early when all contributions and expenditures with respect to the election to which the statement will apply have been made, but the committee will continue to receive and/or make contributions and to make expenditures prior to the closing date established by Section 84205 (albeit with respect to a different election). The main problem created by permitting an early filing is that the committee may close its books early but another com- mittee to which it makes a contribution prior to the closing date might keep its books open until the specified closing date. If this occurs, the transaction would be reported on the recipient committee's postelection statement but not on the donor commit- tee's postelection statement. To the contrary, the donor commit- tee would report the contribution on its first preelection state- ment for the next election. In light of this problem, it was determined that strict adherence to the closing dates specified in the statute is desirable. Accordingly, at the present time, the staff should inform committees that they may not close their books prior to the specified closing date if they anticipate any further contributions or expenditures during the postelection period established by the statute. In the meantime, the staff will investigate the feasibility of interpreting the last sentence of Section 84205 to read, in effect: "if all liabilities of the filer have been paid and no additional contributions or expenditures are anticipated with respect to the election for which the campaign statement is being filed." An interpretation of Section 84205 which reads into the statute the underlined portion of the foregoing quote would permit an early filing under the circumstances outlined above. KF:cjb
embedding.summary: This August 31, 1976 FPPC memorandum addresses whether campaign committees may file postelection statements early under Section 84205 when all activity for one election is complete but additional activity for other elections continues during the statutory closing period. The FPPC concludes that strict adherence to closing dates is required to prevent reporting inconsistencies between donor and recipient committees.
